location.office_time_required: 3-4 days a week in one of our SF Bay Area offices

seniority.level: Senior IC to Principal / Director-level (founder-mode, high autonomy)
level_signals: Founding member of forward deployed team
Works directly with C-suite of strategic customers and Glean's own executive team
Comp range $170K-$280K base is wide, suggesting multiple levels possible
Described as operating with 'autonomy and accountability of a founder'

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 0-to-1 product track record: You've built products from scratch that others have used. You can point to something that didn't exist before you showed up.

Deep technical fluency: You can shape the product vision and architectures, work directly with engineers on building & implementation, and make credible design decisions. You've shipped AI-powered solutions in the real world, not just designed them.
Business acumen: You lead discovery with a point of view, not a list of questions. You can identify where there is opportunity in a business and connect that to a product hypothesis quickly.
Executive credibility: You've built real trust with senior stakeholders by being right about important things and delivering on what you committed to.
Product taste: You design products people actually want to use. You think about the end user's experience with the same rigor you bring to the underlying architecture.
Comfort with ambiguity: You define the problem as often as you solve it, and you're energized by that.

You think like a founder. When you walk into a customer, you're not looking for a use case to fit an existing solution - you're diagnosing the business, forming a hypothesis about where the real value is, and figuring out what needs to exist that doesn't yet.
This role will require travel 25-50% of the time

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Nice to have: Experience as a founder, a top-tier consulting firm or in product at a high-growth technology company.
A technical degree in CS, engineering, math, or a related field.
Deep familiarity with LLMs, retrieval systems, and evaluation frameworks.
Experience driving technology adoption inside large enterprises.

scope.surface: Forward deployed product creation — 0-to-1 new product surfaces extending Glean's Work AI platform for strategic enterprise customers
primary_metrics_inferred: Product adoption and rollout to broader Glean customer base
core_responsibilities: Discover and build Glean's next product surfaces through 0-to-1 product creation
Lead deep opportunity discovery by diagnosing customer business problems
Serve as trusted C-suite advisor at strategic customer organizations
Set product vision for what Glean can become inside customer organizations
Scale successful solutions by contributing to playbooks for the broader team

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed ($170K-$280K base plus equity)
Founding member of a new team — high-visibility, high-ownership opportunity
Direct access to Glean executive team and customer C-suites
Work ships to entire Glean customer base — broad real-world impact
Recognized company (Fast Company Top 10, Forbes AI 50, CNBC Disruptor 50)
Clear articulation of role uniqueness vs. typical PM or solutions roles
red_flags: Extremely wide comp range ($170K-$280K) suggests significant level ambiguity — actual offer could vary substantially
Hybrid 3-4 days/week in SF Bay Area eliminates remote candidates entirely
25-50% travel requirement is substantial and not negotiable per the JD
Role is highly ambiguous by design — requires comfort with near-zero structure
No explicit years-of-experience requirement stated, making it hard to self-assess fit
High buzzword density may obscure actual day-to-day realities of the role

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range, founder framing, and C-suite access)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (not stated in JD; inferred from responsibilities)

ambiguities: Comp range of $170K-$280K is unusually wide; may represent multiple seniority bands or locations rather than a single level. Level is unclear.

The JD lists 'Nice to have: Experience as a founder, a top-tier consulting firm or in product at a high-growth technology company' as a single bullet — treated as a single nice-to-have requirement with multiple acceptable backgrounds.
No explicit years-of-experience minimum stated anywhere in the JD; seniority inferred from context only.
